hero.focus: Distributed systems · Protocol implementations · CLI tooling
hero.status: Open to backend engineering roles · Seoul & remote

about.title:
Self-taught backend engineer
based in Seoul, South Korea.
about.p1: I got into backend engineering by building things I didn't fully understand yet — DNS resolvers, programming language interpreters, distributed caches. That approach stuck. Most of what I know came from reading source code, RFCs, and breaking things until they worked.
about.p2: I care about the layer underneath the framework — how data moves, where it lives, what happens when things fail. I'm most comfortable in Go and TypeScript, and I tend to reach for the simplest solution that actually handles the hard cases.
about.p3: I'm currently looking for backend engineering roles in Seoul and internationally. If something on this site caught your attention, I'd like to hear from you.

contact.title: Get in touch
contact.subtitle: Whether it's a job opportunity, a project, or just a question about something you read here — I'll get back to you.
